meta_title: For Students: SaaS pricing to Solve can't debug code (Advanced Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome can't debug code. A Advanced-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: SaaS Pricing Strategy: Fix Debugging Issues with AI Tools
content.seo_description: Master SaaS pricing and resolve complex debugging bottlenecks. An advanced tutorial for students using Antigravity and Claude Code to build professional apps.
content.answer_block: Debugging failures often stem from poorly defined logic rather than syntax. By decoupling your SaaS pricing tiers into independent, testable modules using Antigravity and Claude Code, you isolate state-dependent errors. This architectural shift forces clear data flow, making invisible bugs impossible to hide and ensuring your revenue logic remains robust.
content.intro:
You’ve been staring at the terminal for hours. The stack trace is a labyrinth, the console is bleeding red, and your SaaS pricing logic—the very heartbeat of your revenue model—is failing silently. It’s the classic 'developer’s wall,' where the complexity of your architecture has outpaced your ability to trace the execution path.

But here is the secret: debugging isn’t about searching for typos; it’s about architectural discipline. By leveraging next-gen AI-assisted development, you can transform these cryptic errors into crystal-clear diagnostic signals. We aren't just fixing a bug; we are rebuilding your development pipeline to ensure your pricing engine is as bulletproof as it is profitable.

steps.title: Architectural Decoupling
steps.description: Isolate the pricing engine from the UI layer to stop state-pollution.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor the pricing calculation module into a pure, stateless function that accepts a user object and returns a calculated tier.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --file ./src/pricing.ts --task 'Isolate pricing logic into a pure function'

steps.title: Automated Unit Constraints
steps.description: Create strict boundary checks for pricing tiers using Antigravity.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate exhaustive edge-case tests for the tiered pricing logic, specifically focusing on negative inputs and overflow values.

steps.title: Observability Injection
steps.description: Inject telemetry to track pricing calculations in real-time.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Add structured logging to the pricing flow that captures input state, tier selection, and final output without altering return types.

steps.title: Simulated Load Testing
steps.description: Stress test your pricing logic to find race conditions.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a stress test script that executes 1,000 concurrent pricing calculations to identify potential state-related race conditions.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Execute high-concurrency pricing stress test'

steps.title: Validation Loop Integration
steps.description: Ensure the system self-corrects invalid pricing inputs.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Implement a Zod-based schema validation layer that rejects invalid pricing data before it reaches the main compute function.

pro_tips: Always treat your pricing logic as a 'black box' that only accepts valid DTOs.
Use Claude Code to document your 'why' in comments, not just the 'what'.
Never debug in production; create a local mock environment that mimics your production database.
Implement 'Feature Flags' for pricing tiers to roll back instantly if a bug hits production.
Focus on 'traceability'—if you can't trace the data path, your architecture is too complex.
content.next_steps: Deploy your refined pricing module to a staging environment and run a smoke test to ensure all edge cases are handled before pushing to production.
content.conversion_block: You now possess the advanced debugging framework used by top-tier SaaS engineers. Don't let your code hold your vision hostage. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
